[package]
name = "ritual-scheduled-verification"
version.workspace = true
edition.workspace = true
authors = ["Ritual Team <team@ritual.net>"]
description = "Scheduler contract state reading for scheduled transaction verification (C1-C10)"

[dependencies]
alloy-primitives = { workspace = true, features = ["serde"] }
thiserror.workspace = true

atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence adds the scheduled-verification crate manifest, defining the Scheduler state-reading and C1-C10 verification crate with Alloy primitive serialization, typed errors, tracing, and REVM database dependencies.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Exact preservation keeps the scheduled-verification crate metadata and minimal REVM-backed dependency surface unchanged.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Removing the REVM dependency should break database-backed scheduled call and predicate reads.

summary: The scheduled-verification manifest is exact-port preserved with the REVM-backed state-reading dependency surface.
